String format_string(const String &oldst.brief: Formats a string by wrapping its content into paragraphs.
String format_string(const String &oldst.details:
Formats a string by wrapping its paragraphs to a specified length.
This function takes a String object and formats it by wrapping its content into paragraphs with a maximum line length of 77 characters. It utilizes the format_page function to perform the wrapping and ensures that each paragraph is separated by a newline character. The function is useful for preparing text for display where line length is constrained.

params.s: The original String object representing the text to be formatted.

String format_string(const String &oldst.returns: A formatted String object containing the wrapped paragraphs, with each paragraph separated by a newline character.
String format_string(const String &oldst.name: format_string

void load_notes().brief: Loads various categories of notes from their respective files into linked lists.
void load_notes().details: The function load_notes() initializes the application's note data by reading stored notes from predefined files. It calls load_thread() for each note category, passing the filename, the address of the linked list head, the note type identifier, and a time threshold. In the current implementation, the time threshold is set to zero, indicating that all notes are loaded regardless of age. Previously, the code included commented-out calls with specific time thresholds to filter out older notes, but these are inactive. This function effectively populates the in-memory linked lists with notes from persistent storage, preparing the application for further operations.
void load_notes().returns: void; the function does not return a value but populates global linked lists with loaded notes.
void load_notes().name: load_notes
